text: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have become a popular choice for modeling structural data in recommender systems. However, real-world recommendation scenarios are often highly complex and involve diverse types of relationship between different data entities, such as social connections between users or semantic attributes between items, which can provide abundant cues to improve user preference learning. In this work, we focus on the challenging task of utilizing heterogeneous graph-enhanced relational learning for improving the performance of recommender systems. To this end, we propose a new framework called Heterogeneous Graph Contrastive Learning (HGCL), which not only connects heterogeneous relational semantics with user-item interaction behaviors but also incorporates contrastive learning to facilitate the knowledge distillation from the diverse graph views. To further improve the learned representation, we adopt meta-networks to achieve the adaptive contrastive augmentation for different side information, making the augmentation process more adaptable to different data patterns. Experiments on three real-world datasets demonstrate that our proposed HGCL model outperforms the existing state-of-the-art models for recommendation. Ablation studies are also conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed components in HGCL. Our implementation of the proposed model is available at https://github.com/HKUDS/HGCL.
